title: Narrative shift from growth to efficiency
description: Real-world PM scenario on narrative shift from growth to efficiency with conflicting stakeholder incentives and incomplete data.
scenarioBrief: You are a PM at AdCrest, a Series C marketing tech company (340 employees, $39.2M ARR). Your immediate assignment is narrative shift from growth to efficiency. The executive team expects a recommendation before Friday's review. You have four context sources: a KPI snapshot, stakeholder notes, customer voice, and execution risk data. Each source is useful but incomplete, and at least one source is biased or contradictory. Choose the smallest plan that can prove value without increasing operational risk.

contextMaterials.title: KPI Snapshot (Last 90 Days)
contextMaterials.content:
Monthly active accounts: 22411
New logo adds: 3546
Activation rate: 32%
30-day retention: 23%
NPS: 1 (n=152)
Expansion rate in top cohort: 19%
Quarter-over-quarter activation delta: 6 points
Quarter-over-quarter retention delta: 5 points
Caveat: NPS sample over-indexes power users and excludes the most recent downgraded accounts.

contextMaterials.title: Stakeholder Notes
contextMaterials.content:
CRO (Asha Nguyen): "I need a plan sales can explain in live calls next week, not a roadmap promise."
VP Engineering (Victor Carter): "We can move quickly if every blocker has an owner and fallback path."
Head of Customer Success (Derek Morris): "Escalations spike after handoff because expectations are set too optimistically."
Commercial note: 5 late-stage deals cite this area as a decision factor.
Support note: 15 escalations in the last month mention related pain points.
Bias warning: projected revenue impact is directional and not contract-backed.

contextMaterials.title: Customer Voice (Interview Snippets)
contextMaterials.content:
Clearwater Finance (Operations Lead): "We see value quickly once configured, but the first session still feels too heavy."
Orbit Travel Co (IT Manager): "A few avoidable errors in onboarding made the team question readiness."
Keystone Legal (Director of Product): "The concept is right, but edge-case handling still feels unfinished."
Data gap: support transcript tagging is inconsistent across segments.

contextMaterials.title: Delivery and Risk Snapshot
contextMaterials.content:
Open P1/P2 issues: 19
Median cycle time: 9 days
On-time delivery last quarter: 76%
Shared platform dependencies unresolved: 9
Release rollback events last quarter: 4
Average time to resolve escalations: 28 hours
Contradiction: roadmap shows full utilization, but squads report 10-20% interrupt-driven bandwidth.

submissionFields.hint: Map each audience to the message they need and why.
submissionFields.label: Audience and Intent

submissionFields.hint: Write the core narrative that aligns stakeholders on action.
submissionFields.label: Core Narrative

submissionFields.hint: Predict objections from key stakeholders and how you will respond.
submissionFields.label: Expected Pushback

submissionFields.hint: Draft the memo or all-hands update you would send.
submissionFields.label: Message Draft

submissionFields.hint: Explain how you will collect feedback and close open issues.
submissionFields.label: Follow-up and Feedback Loop

rubric.name: Audience Fit
rubric.criteria: Score 8-10: Uses specific evidence from at least three context sources, states assumptions, and makes clear trade-offs. Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Is messaging adapted to the needs and incentives of each audience?

Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Is communication clear, direct, and decision-oriented?

rubric.name: Credibility
rubric.criteria: Score 8-10: Uses specific evidence from at least three context sources, states assumptions, and makes clear trade-offs. Score 5-7: Directionally sound but missing precision on evidence, caveats, or execution details. Score 1-4: Generic response with weak linkage to context and constraints.
rubric.description: Are claims grounded in evidence with transparent caveats?

expertSolution.content: Executives need decision logic and downside exposure, delivery teams need concrete scope and ownership, and customer-facing teams need timing and expectation framing. Tailor message depth by audience without changing the core narrative.

Recommendation: align on a staged plan because the data is mixed, not broken. Activation is 32% and retention is 23%, while NPS (1, n=152) and 15 escalations indicate trust risk if execution quality slips.

Expect commercial pressure from Asha Nguyen around the 5 deal pipeline, technical caution from Victor Carter on dependency risk (9 unresolved), and customer-impact pressure from Derek Morris due to escalation volume. Address each with explicit thresholds and review dates.

Team, we are moving to a bounded plan that prioritizes reliability and measurable customer value. Baseline today is 22411 active accounts, activation 32%, retention 23%, and on-time delivery 76%. We will publish weekly progress and revisit scope at the midpoint checkpoint.

Hold a structured Q&A within 24 hours, capture unresolved questions in a decision log, and assign owner plus due date for each item. Publish weekly updates that close the loop on objections raised by Sales, Engineering, and Customer Success.
